Output: EXP-257 — 5-question methodology preface placed BELOW the EXP-255 thesis-hero and ABOVE the accuracy-math-section on the Overview tab. The 5 questions are reused VERBATIM from the existing eg-evaluate section in the Evaluation Guide tab (no invented content per HNOTE 'no derived content' / 'no invented conditions' rule). The medicine-analogy long-form treatment in eg-evaluate is unchanged — preface caption links to it ('Full preface →').

Five question link mapping:
  Q1 'Does the prediction distinguish this model from alternatives?' → #stdmodel (3.4 Detailed: Standard Model Explains, tab 'wins')
  Q2 'Can the prediction be derived from the model's own parameters?' → #selfcon (3.3 Detailed: Self-Contradicted, tab 'wins')
  Q3 'Is the cited data accurately represented?' → #refuted (3.2 Detailed: Refuted by Data, tab 'wins')
  Q4 'Is the claim falsifiable?' → #misleading (3.6 Detailed: Misleading and Unfalsifiable, tab 'wins')
  Q5 'Are the same data being counted multiple times?' → #dedup-analysis (6.6b De-Duplication Analysis, tab 'predictions')

Placement decision (operator offered ABOVE thesis as eyebrow OR below at body weight): chose BELOW at body weight. Rationale — above the thesis is already congested after EXP-256 integration (verdict bar + falsifiability-module = two structured blocks before the thesis); adding a third would create a stacking wall before the punchline. Placing the preface BELOW the thesis preserves it as visual climax of the first viewport, and the methodology serves as 'here's how we got there, and here's how you can re-evaluate' — natural reading order. The 'preface' label still applies because the questions preface the rest of the review (wins, kill-shots, predictions), not the thesis itself.

Visual differentiation from EXP-256 falsifiability-module: deliberately distinct. EXP-256 uses --accent left rail + grid layout + verdict-encoded status badges (review-specific verdict criteria). EXP-257 uses --ink-3 left rail + numbered Q&A + right-aligned section labels (universal methodology, neutral coloring). Reader can distinguish the two structured blocks at a glance — they answer different questions.

Final Overview stack order after all three EXPs integrate: title-block → verdict bar → EXP-256 falsifiability-module → EXP-255 thesis-hero → EXP-257 evaluate-preface → accuracy-math-section → Downloads / Verdict Categories.

Patches: two patch_segments in build-scripts/generate-html.js — EP1 inserts HTML before the unique anchor '<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">' (line ~1388); EP2 inserts CSS after the unique anchor '.verdict-bars .vb-caption{font-size:.9rem;...}' (line ~537). Decider routing: queue-for-curmudgeon-then-integrate per HNOTE explicit directive ('Substantive overview rework. Should route through curmudgeon for adversarial review (NOT auto-integrate)'). With EXP-255 already routed to curmudgeon (qid known) and EXP-256 routed at 2026-04-26T03:25:32Z, EXP-257 now joins the queue. The trio should ideally be reviewed and integrated in sequence to maintain Overview composition coherence.

Curmudgeon review focus list embedded in proposal (9 items): question-verbatim verification, link-target demonstrative-value audit (with alternative-rejection rationale to stress-test), placement stress-test (BELOW vs ABOVE thesis), redundancy stress-test with EXP-256 falsifiability-module, q4 link-target stress-test (#misleading vs #eg-falsifiability), cross-tab link UX (q5 jumps overview→predictions), responsive @media check, DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 design rubric, anchor stability re-verification at review time.

Counterargument considered (per user-preferences directive): the strongest defender objection is that adding a fifth structured block to the Overview risks turning it into 'a wall of widgets'. Rebuttal embedded in EXP-257.anticipated_objections[0]: each block has a distinct rhetorical job and a deliberately distinct visual treatment (5 different visual vocabularies across the 5 blocks); the reader can stop at any depth and have read a coherent argument. A second defender objection is that question 4 ('Is the claim falsifiable?') should link to #eg-falsifiability not #misleading. Rebuttal in EXP-257.anticipated_objections[1]: the question is about evaluating dome claims, so it should link to the dome-claim bucket; #eg-falsifiability is already linked twice from caption paths to avoid redirect collision. Curmudgeon explicitly asked to stress-test both.

Kernel of truth (per analyst directive): the round-1 dashboard treatment with stat tiles WAS load-bearing for credibility — readers need scannable signals on first viewport before committing to long-form prose. The 5-question preface honors that by being a scannable methodology guide — five short questions, each a clickable shortcut to where the failure mode is demonstrated. It does NOT recreate the dashboard-feel (deliberately removed by EXP-255), but it gives readers the same affordance: orient quickly, drill in only where you want to.
